tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Free/Pro tiers — wire Stripe subscription billing ($19.99/mo)
tasks.description:
DEFERRED 2026-04-29 (post-launch). Reasoning: amplifiers with no earnings track record cannot rationally evaluate a $19.99/mo subscription — Pro converts post-traction users, which MVP hasn't produced yet. The 20% platform cut is sufficient MVP monetization. Free 4 posts/day cap also dropped (only made sense as upgrade friction). Reputation tier (seedling/grower/amplifier) still governs campaign count + 2x earnings multiplier.

Revisit triggers: (a) amplifier cohort consistently earning $300+/month, (b) campaign-supply scarcity making match-priority valuable, (c) feature demand that genuinely costs money to deliver (premium AI models, advanced analytics, faster scrape).

ORIGINAL SCOPE (preserved for revival):
Users (amplifiers) subscribe to a Pro plan ($19.99/month) that unlocks premium features. Separate from reputation tiers (seedling/grower/amplifier).

Tier comparison: Free → 4 posts/day, standard match priority. Pro → 20 posts/day, +20% match score boost, max(reputation_limit, 20) campaigns. Image generation enabled on both. Metric scrape frequency same (24h).

Flow: Settings → 'Upgrade to Pro' → Stripe Checkout (subscription mode) → webhook checkout.session.completed sets subscription_tier=pro, subscription_status=active.

Webhooks: checkout.session.completed (activate), invoice.payment_succeeded (extend), invoice.payment_failed (7-day grace then revert), customer.subscription.deleted (revert to free).

Feature gates: post-count gate before scheduling, max_campaigns = max(reputation, pro_20), matching priority *= 1.2 if Pro.

Edge cases: admin-suspended Pro user keeps active sub but locked out of platform; payment fail = 7-day grace; mid-period upgrade applies immediately; cancellation at period end.

DORMANT SCAFFOLDING already in repo (left in place, inert with default 'free'):
- server/app/models/user.py:53 — subscription_tier column, default 'free'
- server/app/routers/users.py:53–56 — PATCH accepts subscription_tier (validates free|pro)
- server/app/schemas/user.py:13,28 — subscription_tier field on update schema
- server/app/services/billing.py:39–67 — SUBSCRIPTION_TIERS dict + get_effective_max_campaigns() (returns reputation limit while everyone is free)

NOT YET BUILT (to do on revival): Stripe subscription checkout endpoint, webhook handler, post-limit enforcement, matching score multiplier, settings page upgrade UI.

7 ACs in original spec — see git history for docs/specs/batch-4-business-launch.md before commit removing Task #17 section.

metadata.deferred_reason: Cold-start economics: pre-traction amplifiers can't rationally evaluate $19.99/mo. 20% cut is sufficient MVP monetization.

tasks.title: Plan + execute Vercel migration (target host undecided)
tasks.description: Server offline after Vercel billed ~$47/cycle (serverless model incompatible with Amplifier's high poll volume from user apps). Project deleted, billing disputed. Migration path NOT YET DECIDED — still in planning. Full planning doc with analysis, options, and step-by-step plan: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md. Leading candidate is shared Hostinger KVM VPS (owned by Nili+Daniel, co-hosts TTE+Stock Buddy) but not confirmed. Fallback options include Render flat-fee and Railway.
tasks.details:
READ FIRST: docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md — 12 sections covering options analysis, server requirements, step-by-step setup, env vars, client cutover, Vercel cleanup (~62 files), cutover sequence, rollback, cost comparison, follow-ups, and open questions. Before executing: (1) decide target host (Hostinger VPS vs Render vs Railway vs other), (2) confirm with Nili/Daniel if going Hostinger route — KVM tier, SSH access, existing Caddy/Redis/UFW, subdomain, (3) audit ENCRYPTION_KEY usage before rotating, (4) verify ARQ WorkerSettings entrypoint exists. Clients still pointing to dead Vercel URL via config/.env:7 and scripts/utils/server_client.py:22. Server is OFFLINE right now — users can't access anything until this ships.

=== HOSTING DECISION (added 2026-04-25 after father review) ===
Three viable options compared. Recommended: Option B (existing shared Hostinger VPS).

Option A — New free Vercel account: REJECTED. Same trap. Serverless billing scales with traffic;
  Amplifier's 10-min poll loop from every user app instance will burn the free tier quickly and
  generate the same surprise charge that killed the original project ($47/cycle).

Option B — Existing Hostinger KVM VPS (shared with TTE + Stock Buddy, owned by Daniel + Nili):
  RECOMMENDED. Flat fee already paid (sunk cost). Has Caddy + Redis + UFW configured. Supports
  systemd + ARQ workers cleanly. Requires SSH access handoff from Nili. Risk: shared resource
  contention with TTE (1300+ symbols scaling to 14k) and Stock Buddy. If contention becomes an
  issue, fall back to Option C.

Option C — Second Hostinger account (separate VPS dedicated to Amplifier): FALLBACK. Pros: no
  shared-resource concern, full control. Cons: extra ongoing cost, extra ops surface. Use only if
  Amplifier outgrows shared resources or Daniel/Nili want isolation.

DECISION ORDER:
  1. Start with Option B. Get SSH access from Nili. Deploy via the migration steps already in
     docs/MIGRATION-FROM-VERCEL.md. ~2-3 hours of work.
  2. Monitor resource usage for first 4 weeks. If stable, stay on B.
  3. If contention shows up (CPU steal, DB slowdowns from neighbors), migrate to Option C.

Option A (new free Vercel) is OFF the table — same failure mode.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Shared research pool — campaigns share research across users
tasks.description: When the 4-phase content agent does research for a campaign on one user's machine, push that research to the server so other users assigned to the same campaign can read it instead of re-doing the work. Forms a collective research pool per campaign that all assigned users contribute to and consume from.
tasks.details:
CONTEXT — current behaviour (Task #14, shipped):
- The content agent's Phase 1 (Research) runs on the user's machine. It scrapes the company's URLs
  (up to 3), reads campaign images via vision AI, searches the web for recent niche news, and
  synthesizes a 'research context' (product summary, key features, content angles, emotional
  hooks, recent news headlines, etc.).
- This research is cached LOCALLY for 7 days (per scripts/utils/content_generator.py).
- Every other user on the same campaign re-does the exact same work on their own machine.
- Wasteful: identical Gemini calls, identical web scrapes, identical token cost.

GOAL — turn it into a shared pool:
1. After research finishes locally, agent uploads the research blob to the server keyed by
   (campaign_id, research_version). Server stores it on a new CampaignResearch table.
2. Before doing research locally, agent first asks server: 'do you have research for campaign X
   that is < 7 days old?'. If yes, downloads and caches locally. If partial (e.g., URLs scraped
   but no news search done yet), agent does only the missing parts and uploads the delta.
3. Research is split into discrete sections (product_scrape, image_analysis, niche_news,
   emotional_hooks, content_angles). Each section is independently versionable so partial
   research is mergeable.
4. Server merges contributions from multiple users — last-write-wins per section, but the union
   of all sections forms the final cached pool.

SCHEMA additions (new model):
- CampaignResearch: id, campaign_id (FK), section ('product_scrape' | 'image_analysis' |
  'niche_news' | 'emotional_hooks' | 'content_angles'), payload (JSON), contributed_by_user_id,
  created_at, expires_at
- Index: (campaign_id, section, created_at desc) for fast 'latest fresh research' lookup

API additions (new endpoints):
- GET  /api/campaigns/{id}/research → returns latest non-expired sections, merged
- POST /api/campaigns/{id}/research → upload a section payload

AGENT changes (scripts/utils/content_generator.py):
- Before each research section: GET from server, use if fresh
- After each research section: POST to server with payload + section name
- Local 7-day cache becomes a fallback if server is unreachable

BENEFITS:
- Token cost per campaign drops from O(users × research) to O(research)
- Faster onboarding for late-joiners — research already cached
- Companies could optionally see the research pool (could become a 'transparency' feature later)

EDGE CASES:
- Race conditions: two users research the same section at the same time → last-write-wins is fine
- Stale research: 7-day TTL expires → next user redoes it; subsequent users benefit again
- Privacy: nothing user-identifying in research payloads (just product/market info), safe to share
- Trust: a malicious user could inject bad research. Mitigation: server-side AI can validate or
  flag obviously-junk research before caching it (post-MVP refinement)
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Two users (User A, User B) accept the same campaign within minutes of each other.
2. User A's agent runs research first → uploads 5 sections to server.
3. User B's agent starts research second → expect server returns existing sections, B does NO
   Gemini calls, downloads research, generates content using A's research.

tasks.title: [DEFERRED] Add Nvidia free-tier as 4th AI provider fallback
tasks.description:

Free-tier gives single key access to 80+ LLMs including MiniMax, GLM-5, DeepSeek, Kimi, Nemotron. Rate limit ~40 RPM, no daily/monthly cap.

DEFERRED 2026-04-29 — current 3-provider chain (Gemini + Mistral + Groq) works in production after Task #15 verification. No known problem this would solve.

Caveats per Grok analysis (full doc: docs/research/nvidia-free-tier-for-amplifier.md):
- Trial terms restrict to prototyping/testing only, not production
- Terms prohibit "providing a service to others" — even with BYOK, Amplifier proxying may violate spirit of terms
- User onboarding adds Nvidia Developer Program signup + possible phone verification
- No SLA, can be throttled or suspended under load

Revisit triggers:
1. Gemini+Mistral+Groq chain fails consistently over a 1-week production window
2. Onboarding friction data shows users lack any of the existing 3 provider keys
3. Nvidia changes terms to explicitly allow BYOK consumer-app proxying
4. Nvidia-only model (Nemotron etc) becomes critical for a feature

Implementation sketch: add nvidia_provider.py to scripts/ai/ following provider.py base class pattern. OpenAI-compatible HTTP calls. Read NVIDIA_API_KEY from settings (encrypted in user-side local.db settings table). Server-side: extend server/app/services/quality_gate.py provider chain.

tasks.title: Comprehensive UAT — user app, company dashboard, admin dashboard
tasks.description: Pre-launch UAT gate. Drive every page, every form, every button across all three user-facing surfaces (local user app at :5222, company dashboard at /company/*, admin dashboard at /admin/*). Catch console errors, broken empty states, dead buttons, copy bugs, missing CTAs, and any feature that ships but doesn't actually work end-to-end.
tasks.details: Three sub-surfaces, each with its own Verification Procedure block in docs/specs/ following docs/uat/AC-FORMAT.md. (1) USER APP: onboarding, login, campaigns tab (open invitations / active / declined), drafts review (text + image), approve+post flow with real platform post then delete, earnings page (summary + per-campaign + history), withdraw flow (with and without Stripe Connect), settings (platforms, API keys, region, mode), background agent control (start/stop, status). (2) COMPANY DASHBOARD: register, login, dashboard cards, campaigns list + filters, create campaign (manual + AI wizard), campaign detail (assignments, posts, metrics, decline reasons, edit, activate, complete, cancel, refund), billing top-up (Stripe test card), influencers list, stats, settings. (3) ADMIN DASHBOARD: login, overview, users + user detail, companies + company detail, campaigns + campaign detail, financial (run-billing, run-payout, run-earning-promotion, run-payout-processing, void payout, force-approve payout), fraud, analytics, review queue (approve/reject queued campaigns), audit log filters, settings. Use Chrome DevTools MCP for verification (snapshot DOM, list_console_messages, list_network_requests, screenshots). Use Playwright only for the real-platform-posting AC. Refuses 'done' until: every page loads with zero console errors, every CTA leads to expected next state, audit_log has zero severity=error during the run, every screenshot embedded in report. Estimated ~50-70 ACs total across the 3 sub-tasks.
tasks.testStrategy: Three /uat-task runs, one per surface. Each writes its own report to docs/uat/reports/task-74-<sub>-<date>.md. Aggregate PASS rule: all 3 sub-task reports show every AC PASS, zero error-level console messages across all pages, zero severity=error rows in audit_log during the UAT window.

tasks.title: Web onboarding flow — register page + 4 onboarding steps + auth callback wiring
tasks.description:
LAUNCH BLOCKER. The Phase D creator-app-split migration spec'd a 16-step web-driven onboarding flow (migration-creator-app-split.md lines 102-120) but the implementation only shipped the local /auth/callback endpoint. The hosted server has NO public register page, NO /onboarding/step2/3/4 routes, and NO /user/onboarding redirect target. Result: a fresh user literally cannot sign up — login.html says 'Install the desktop app and complete onboarding to create one' but scripts/onboarding.py doesn't exist either. Surfaced during Task #74 launch UAT prep when curl-registering uat-user-74 bypassed the broken UI flow.

File changes:
- New `server/app/routers/onboarding.py` with: GET /register (HTML), POST /register (creates user, generates JWT, redirects to localhost:5222/auth/callback?token=...), GET /onboarding/step2 (Connect platforms — links to localhost:5222/connect + SSE-driven badge from /sse/user/agent-status), GET /onboarding/step3 (Set up AI keys — link to localhost:5222/keys), GET /onboarding/step4 (Done — redirect to /user/campaigns), GET /user/onboarding (redirect to step2 — fixes local_server.py:177 dead-end).
- New templates: server/app/templates/auth/register.html, onboarding/step2.html, onboarding/step3.html, onboarding/step4.html.
- Update server/app/templates/user/login.html — replace 'Install the desktop app...' with 'Sign Up' link to /register?agent=true.
- Update scripts/utils/local_server.py:177 — change redirect target to /onboarding/step2 (explicit) instead of /user/onboarding.
- Mount new router in server/app/main.py.
- ToS checkbox required on /register (per Task #28 pattern).
tasks.details: Spec source: docs/migrations/2026-04-28-migration-creator-app-split.md lines 102-120 (16-step Auth handoff sequence). Existing patterns to reuse: company /register flow at server/app/routers/company/login.py:53 + ToS gate at server/app/routers/auth.py:19 + JWT token creation already exists. Key edge cases: duplicate email (409), ToS not checked (400), invalid token in callback (400), user closes browser between steps (resume on next /user/login).
tasks.testStrategy: /uat-task 75 with user monitoring first run. ~10-12 ACs covering: register page renders, POST /register creates user + sets JWT cookie + redirects, JWT lands in local SQLite encrypted, /onboarding/step2 shows pending platform connections, completing one platform via local /connect updates SSE badge, step3 links to local /keys, step4 redirects to /user/campaigns, edge cases (duplicate email, ToS unchecked, invalid token, abandoned flow). tasks.details: RTF format quirks: use `{\rtf1\ansi\deff0 ... }` shell, `\par` for paragraph breaks, `\b ... \b0` for bold. Keep formatting simple; Inno Setup's RTF renderer is conservative.
